{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Benko Gambit| |moves=1.d4 Nf6 2.c4 c5 3.d5 b5 4.g4 |eco=[[Chess/ECOA|A57-A59]] |parent=[[Chess/Benoni Defence|Benoni Defence]] }} = Benko Gambit: Mutkin Countergambit = If Black accepts the free g-pawn, White can gain center control with 5. e4 while Black's knight remains in an unfavorable position. If Black ignores the gambit, 5. g5 will also force Black's knight away from the center. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1. d4 Nf6 2. c4 c5 3. d5 b5 4. g4'''
